content: Migrating JSON blob to relational table pitfalls in Laravel: (1) Race condition during backfill — production traffic writes to JSON while backfill script reads stale data, rows get lost. (2) Eloquent cache layer — models cached with old JSON data persist after schema change; must clear model cache. (3) N+1 queries — new relational table requires eager loading where JSON was inline. (4) Stancl tenancy data column is special — it is used by the tenancy package for tenant identification and context; modifying its structure or removing keys requires understanding the tenancy lifecycle. (5) The TenantModuleRegistry uses last-write-wins via save() on the tenant model; moving to a dedicated table means changing every call site that touches installed_modules and module_operations. (6) Queued jobs that reference old JSON format will fail after cutover unless deployed in correct order.
